danger_signals.visual_form: Mutant tentacle creature with flipper-like arms; visual distinction from Green Tentacle
danger_signals.meaning: Primary antagonist threat; indicates world under control/domination

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental storytelling in future timeline showing controlled populace
danger_signals.meaning: World-state danger: Purple Tentacle's successful domination scenario

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual indicator of contamination in river behind Dr. Fred's laboratory
danger_signals.meaning: Source of danger; catalyst for Purple Tentacle mutation and intelligence gain

danger_signals.visual_form: Broken Chron-o-John machines; temporal displacement events
danger_signals.meaning: Danger from uncontrolled time travel consequences; characters separated across timelines

safety_signals.visual_form: Harmless, friendly brother tentacle; visual contrast to Purple variant
safety_signals.meaning: Allied entity; seeks help; non-hostile faction marker

safety_signals.visual_form: Safe haven location; central hub with Chron-o-John machines
safety_signals.meaning: Base of operations; puzzle-solving environment; controlled space

safety_signals.visual_form: Point-and-click adventure UI; humorous character design and interactions
safety_signals.meaning: Tonal safety signal: game establishes lighthearted, puzzle-focused atmosphere despite stakes

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Tentacle with flipper-like arms; mutant appendage
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: In future timeline: appears as world ruler; enslaver of humanity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Future timeline (200 years ahead) shows world under Purple Tentacle rule from Edison mansion; enslaved population

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Harmless tentacle; original form/brother to Purple
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Associated with plea for help; allied with Bernard Bernoulli; represents resistance to Purple's takeover

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Three playable characters: Bernard (nerd), Laverne (deranged medical student), Hoagie (roadie)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cover art shows trio running from tentacle antagonist; dispersed across three time periods

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mad scientist archetype; creator of tentacle mutations and time machines
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Laboratory and Edison motel serve as base; initially intends to kill Purple Tentacle; provides time travel solution

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Green Tentacle as harmless assistant; normal Edison motel/laboratory operations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Green Tentacle friendly and cooperative; Purple Tentacle not yet mutated; no global threat

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Post-sludge mutation (present day - start of game)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Purple Tentacle now visually distinct: flipper-like arms, increased intelligence indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Purple Tentacle actively pursuing world domination; Dr. Fred forced to take countermeasures; protagonists must intervene

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Future timeline (200 years ahead - if Purple Tentacle succeeds)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: World enslaved under Purple Tentacle rule from Edison mansion; visual representation of dystopian control
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Humanity subjugated; Purple Tentacle operates as world ruler; environment reflects total domination scenario

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Past timeline (200 years before - colonial America period)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cartoon versions of American historical figures (John Hancock, Thomas Jefferson, George Washington); period-appropriate environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Characters interact with historical figures; puzzle solutions affect present through time stream manipulation

environmental_storytelling.technique: Timeline separation through visual environment design
environmental_storytelling.example: Future Edison mansion shows enslaved humanity and Purple Tentacle rule; past timeline shows colonial America with historical figures; present shows pre-takeover state

environmental_storytelling.technique: Causality visualization through object aging and transformation
environmental_storytelling.example: Medical chart in past becomes American flag design in future; items placed in past timelines are discovered in future by other characters; shows temporal consequence chain
Purple Tentacle's newfound flipper-like arms and visible intelligence increase serve as environmental threat markers; physical transformation indicates escalated danger
Purple vs. Green tentacle instantly communicates antagonist vs. ally through primary color distinction; no ambiguity in faction alignment
Edison motel/laboratory as hub shows puzzle solution state through environmental changes across time periods; broken Chron-o-John machines signal narrative inciting incident

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Actively pursuing world domination; escape from Dr. Fred's control; displays increased intelligence and physical capability
Controls entire world from Edison mansion; enslaves humanity; demonstrates absolute power and success in takeover
Friendly and cooperative; sends plea for help to Bernard; resists evil brother; allied with protagonists
Responds to Green Tentacle's plea; travels to Edison motel; takes action to prevent catastrophe
Plans tentacle elimination (killing both Purple and Green); shifts to time travel solution when intervention fails

notes: Day of the Tentacle uses cartoon-style point-and-click adventure mechanics to communicate world-state through time-travel puzzle design. Danger is primarily signaled through Purple Tentacle's visual mutation (color, physical transformation to flipper-like arms) and the environmental manifestation of its success (future timeline enslavement). The game employs color-coding (Purple vs. Green) for faction identity with minimal ambiguity. Temporal mechanics allow visual representation of consequence chains (objects aging, flag design origin story). The game's comedic tone through cartoon aesthetics provides tonal safety signals that temper the existential stakes of world domination. Environmental storytelling relies on location changes across timelines to show cause-and-effect relationships.
